Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code). Session 005b6edf-85d8-4980-9e17-486b6b8bed3f.

Refs #10965

PR3 of the M2 series ‚Äî third per-server consumer of the BaseServer scaffold (after PR1 [#10966](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10966) merged and PR2 [#10973](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10973) in review).

Evidence: L1 (static syntax + 39 existing github-workflow unit tests all passing locally in 1.6s).

## What ships

### `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/Server.mjs` migration: 226 ‚Üí 110 lines (~51% reduction)

**Removed (inherited from BaseServer):**
- `setupRequestHandlers()` ‚Äî full ListTools/CallTool/result-formatting boilerplate (~100 lines)
- `initAsync()` boilerplate ‚Äî uses default canonical sequence
- Inline `mcpServer` construction ‚Üí uses BaseServer `createMcpServer()` driven by `getServerMetadata()`
- Transport stdio connect ‚Äî inherited

**Kept (github-workflow-specific overrides):**
- `logStartupStatus(health)` ‚Äî gh-CLI-tip on unhealthy + degraded/healthy paths
- `afterHealthcheck(health)` ‚Äî conditionally pre-fetches + caches GitHub viewer permission when health is not unhealthy

**Added (BaseServer override hooks):**
- `getServerMetadata()` ‚Üí `{name: 'neo-github-workflow', version, capabilities}`
- `getToolService()` ‚Üí `{listTools, callTool}` from `services/toolService.mjs`
- `getDependentServices()` ‚Üí `[SyncService]`
- `getHealthService()` ‚Üí `HealthService`
- `getHealthExemptTools()` ‚Üí `['healthcheck']`

## Cleaner than PR2

PR2 (knowledge-base) needed a new BaseServer hook (`onHealthGateFailure`) for KBRecorderService telemetry preservation. PR3 (github-workflow) needs **no new hooks** ‚Äî the existing override surface covers all per-server semantics:

- Health-exempt list: github-workflow openapi.yaml declares one canonical `/healthcheck` operation; `getHealthExemptTools()` returning `['healthcheck']` is equivalent to the original substring match (`name.includes('healthcheck')`).
- Permission-fetch order: `afterHealthcheck(health)` hook fires after healthcheck and before `connectTransport`; preserves the original `if (health.status !== 'unhealthy') { await RepositoryService.fetchAndCacheViewerPermission(); }` semantic exactly.

This validates that the abstraction designed in PR1 holds up across consumers with different telemetry-shape requirements.

## Per-server migration sequence

- ‚úÖ **PR1** [#10966](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10966) ‚Äî BaseServer scaffold + tests (merged)
- üîÑ **PR2** [#10973](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10973) ‚Äî knowledge-base/Server.mjs migration (in review)
- üÜï **PR3 (this)** ‚Äî github-workflow/Server.mjs migration
- üîú **PR4** ‚Äî neural-link/Server.mjs migration (212 LOC; transport-before-services order ‚Äî likely surfaces another hook)
- üîú **PR5** ‚Äî memory-core/Server.mjs migration (580 LOC, most complex ‚Äî stdio identity resolution, sibling-concurrency log, wake substrate)
- üîú **PR6** ‚Äî file-system/Server.mjs migration (149 LOC, Tier-2 local-only)

## Test Evidence

- `node --check ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/Server.mjs` ‚Üí passed
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/ --reporter=list` ‚Üí 39 passed (1.6s)

## Coordination Notes

- This PR depends on PR1 (#10966 BaseServer scaffold, merged in `dev`). Independent of PR2 ‚Äî github-workflow doesn't need the `onHealthGateFailure` hook PR2 adds.
- If PR2 lands first, PR3 has access to the additional hook (unused, harmless). If PR3 lands first, PR2's hook addition still applies cleanly to its branch.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] github-workflow MCP server boots cleanly with the migrated `Server.mjs` extension shape (CI integration row covers; deployment smoke against a Docker-capable runner confirms).

> ### Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge:** The PR prose says the permission-fetch order is preserved ‚Äúexactly.‚Äù Mechanically, the new BaseServer default runs `waitForDependentServices()` before `runHealthcheckAndLogStatus()` / `afterHealthcheck()`, while the old github-workflow server fetched viewer permission before `SyncService.ready()`. I do not consider this blocking because `SyncService.runFullSync()` calls `RepositoryService.getViewerPermission()` on demand before auto-push decisions, so the permission behavior is preserved even if the prefetch point moves. This is a phrasing precision note for future reviews, not a required action here.
>
